2137            max_val: New maximum value.
2138        """
2139        min_val = self.evaluate_value(min_val)
2140        max_val = self.evaluate_value(max_val)
2141
2142        self._validate_limit_one("min", min_val)
2143        self._validate_limit_one("max", max_val)
2144
2145        if max_val < min_val:
2146            raise ValueError("max_val must be greater than min_val")
2147
2148        self._min_value = min_val
2149        self._max_value = max_val
2150
2151        new_cur_lo, new_cur_up = self._clamp_pair_to_range(
2152            (self._current_lower, self._current_upper),
2153            min_val,
2154            max_val,
2155        )
2156        cur_clamped = False
2157        if new_cur_lo != self._current_lower or new_cur_up != self._current_upper:
2158            self._current_lower = new_cur_lo
2159            self._current_upper = new_cur_up
2160            cur_clamped = True
2161
2162        new_dft_lo, new_dft_up = self._clamp_pair_to_range(
2163            (self._default_lower, self._default_upper),
2164            min_val,
2165            max_val,
2166        )
2167        dft_clamped = False
2168        if new_dft_lo != self._default_lower or new_dft_up != self._default_upper:
2169            self._default_lower = new_dft_lo
2170            self._default_upper = new_dft_up
2171            dft_clamped = True
2172
2173        self._slider.blockSignals(True)
2174        try:
2175            self._slider.setRange(float(min_val), float(max_val))
2176            self._slider.setValues((float(self._current_lower), float(self._current_upper)))
2177        finally:
2178            self._slider.blockSignals(False)
2179
2180        self._update_all_labels()
2181        self._update_value_width()
2182
2183        if cur_clamped:
2184            self.valueChanged.emit(self.get_value())
2185
2186        if dft_clamped:
2187            self.defaultChanged.emit(self.get_default_value())
2188
2189    def set_min_value(self, min_val: TNum) -> None:
2190        """Set only the minimum bound.
2191
2192        Args:
2193            min_val: New minimum value.
2194        """
2195        self.set_range(min_val, self._max_value)
2196
2197    def set_max_value(self, max_val: TNum) -> None:
2198        """Set only the maximum bound.
2199
2200        Args:
2201            max_val: New maximum value.
2202        """
2203        self.set_range(self._min_value, max_val)
2204
2205
2206class IntRangeSlider(NumericRangeSliderBase[int]):
2207    """Integer-valued range slider widget."""
2208
2209    def __init__(
2210        self,
2211        label: str,
2212        min_val: int,
2213        max_val: int,
2214        unit: str = "",
2215        init_vals: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = None,
2216        val_fmt: str = "{:d}",
2217        min_limit: int | None = None,
2218        max_limit: int | None = None,
2219        min_limit_inclusive: bool = True,
2220        max_limit_inclusive: bool = True,
2221        use_margins: bool = False,
2222        margins: Tuple[int, int] = (10, 10),
2223        show_range: bool = True,
2224        orientation: Qt.Orientation = Qt.Horizontal,
2225        editable: bool = True,
2226        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,
2227    ):
2228        """Initialize the integer range slider.
2229
2230        Args:
2231            label: Slider label text.
2232            min_val: Minimum finite value.
2233            max_val: Maximum finite value.
2234            unit: Optional unit label.
2235            init_vals: Initial lower and upper values.
2236            val_fmt: Integer display format string.
2237            min_limit: Optional lower hard limit.
2238            max_limit: Optional upper hard limit.
2239            min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower hard limit is inclusive.
2240            max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper hard limit is inclusive.
2241            use_margins: Whether the slider starts in margins mode.
2242            margins: Initial lower and upper margins.
2243            show_range: Whether range labels and ticks should be shown.
2244            orientation: Slider orientation.
2245            editable: Whether configuration editing is enabled.
2246            parent: Optional parent widget.
2247        """
2248        super().__init__(
2249            label=label,
2250            unit=unit,
2251            value_is_int=True,
2252            min_val=min_val,
2253            max_val=max_val,
2254            init_vals=init_vals,
2255            val_fmt=val_fmt,
2256            min_limit=min_limit,
2257            max_limit=max_limit,
2258            min_limit_inclusive=min_limit_inclusive,
2259            max_limit_inclusive=max_limit_inclusive,
2260            use_margins=use_margins,
2261            margins=margins,
2262            show_range=show_range,
2263            orientation=orientation,
2264            editable=editable,
2265            parent=parent,
2266        )
2267
2268    def evaluate_value(self, value: int) -> int:
2269        """Normalize an integer value using the evaluation format.
2270
2271        Args:
2272            value: Integer value to normalize.
2273
2274        Returns:
2275            Normalized integer value.
2276        """
2277        try:
2278            return int(self._eval_fmt.format(value))
2279        except Exception:
2280            return int(value)
2281
2282
2283class FloatRangeSlider(NumericRangeSliderBase[float]):
2284    """Floating-point range slider widget."""
2285
2286    def __init__(
2287        self,
2288        label: str,
2289        min_val: float,
2290        max_val: float,
2291        unit: str = "",
2292        init_vals: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None,
2293        val_fmt: str = "{:.3f}",
2294        min_limit: float | None = None,
2295        max_limit: float | None = None,
2296        min_limit_inclusive: bool = True,
2297        max_limit_inclusive: bool = True,
2298        use_margins: bool = False,
2299        margins: Tuple[int, int] = (10, 10),
2300        show_range: bool = True,
2301        orientation: Qt.Orientation = Qt.Horizontal,
2302        editable: bool = True,
2303        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,
2304    ):
2305        """Initialize the floating-point range slider.
2306
2307        Args:
2308            label: Slider label text.
2309            min_val: Minimum finite value.
2310            max_val: Maximum finite value.
2311            unit: Optional unit label.
2312            init_vals: Initial lower and upper values.
2313            val_fmt: Float display format string.
2314            min_limit: Optional lower hard limit.
2315            max_limit: Optional upper hard limit.
2316            min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower hard limit is inclusive.
2317            max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper hard limit is inclusive.
2318            use_margins: Whether the slider starts in margins mode.
2319            margins: Initial lower and upper margins.
2320            show_range: Whether range labels and ticks should be shown.
2321            orientation: Slider orientation.
2322            editable: Whether configuration editing is enabled.
2323            parent: Optional parent widget.
2324        """
2325        super().__init__(
2326            label=label,
2327            unit=unit,
2328            value_is_int=False,
2329            min_val=min_val,
2330            max_val=max_val,
2331            init_vals=init_vals,
2332            val_fmt=val_fmt,
2333            min_limit=min_limit,
2334            max_limit=max_limit,
2335            min_limit_inclusive=min_limit_inclusive,
2336            max_limit_inclusive=max_limit_inclusive,
2337            use_margins=use_margins,
2338            margins=margins,
2339            show_range=show_range,
2340            orientation=orientation,
2341            editable=editable,
2342            parent=parent,
2343        )
2344
2345    def evaluate_value(self, value: float) -> float:
2346        """Normalize a float value using the evaluation format.
2347
2348        Args:
2349            value: Float value to normalize.
2350
2351        Returns:
2352            Normalized float value.
2353        """
2354        try:
2355            return float(self._eval_fmt.format(float(value)))
2356        except Exception:
2357            return float(value)
2358
2359
2360def _demo_main() -> int:
2361    """Run this module as a standalone demo.
2362
2363    Returns:
2364        Qt application exit code.
2365    """
2366    import sys
2367
2368    from PySide6.QtCore import Qt
2369    from PySide6.QtWidgets import QMainWindow
2370
2371    from settings.app_style import set_app_style
2372
2373    app = QApplication(sys.argv)
2374    set_app_style(app)
2375
2376    win = QMainWindow()
2377    win.setWindowTitle("DualHandleSlider test")
2378
2379    central = QWidget()
2380    outer_layout = QVBoxLayout(central)
2381
2382    enable_cb = QCheckBox("Enable")
2383    enable_cb.setChecked(True)
2384
2385    r1 = IntRangeSlider("n", 0, 100, init_vals=(20, 80))
2386    r2 = FloatRangeSlider("k", 5.0e4, 6.0e4, val_fmt="{:.8S}")
2387    r3 = FloatSlider("A_C", 0.0, 100.0)
2388    r4 = FloatRangeSlider(
2389        "B_D",
2390        0.0,
2391        100.0,
2392        init_vals=(30, 60),
2393        val_fmt="{:.1f}",
2394        orientation=Qt.Vertical,
2395    )
2396    r5 = FloatSlider("C_E", 0.0, 100.0, orientation=Qt.Vertical)
2397
2398    def on_check(checked: bool):
2399        """Enable or disable all demo sliders.
2400
2401        Args:
2402            checked: Checkbox state from Qt.
2403        """
2404        checked = enable_cb.isChecked()
2405        r1.setEnabled(checked)
2406        r2.setEnabled(checked)
2407        r3.setEnabled(checked)
2408        r4.setEnabled(checked)
2409        r5.setEnabled(checked)
2410
2411    enable_cb.stateChanged.connect(on_check)
2412
2413    sliders_layout = QHBoxLayout()
2414
2415    left_layout = QVBoxLayout()
2416    left_layout.addWidget(r1)
2417    left_layout.addWidget(r2)
2418    left_layout.addWidget(r3)
2419
2420    right_layout = QHBoxLayout()
2421    right_layout.addWidget(r4)
2422    right_layout.addWidget(r5)
2423
2424    sliders_layout.addLayout(left_layout, 1)
2425    sliders_layout.addLayout(right_layout)
2426
2427    outer_layout.addWidget(enable_cb)
2428    outer_layout.addLayout(sliders_layout)
2429
2430    win.setCentralWidget(central)
2431    win.resize(600, 300)
2432    win.show()
2433
2434    return app.exec()
2435
2436
2437if __name__ == "__main__":
2438    raise SystemExit(_demo_main())
def make_line_edit( value_is_int: bool, init_value: ~TNum, fmt: str, min_limit: ~TNum, max_limit: ~TNum, min_limit_inclusive: bool, max_limit_inclusive: bool) -> ui.numeric_line_edit.NumericLineEdit: 
 96def make_line_edit(
 97    value_is_int: bool,
 98    init_value: TNum,
 99    fmt: str,
100    min_limit: TNum,
101    max_limit: TNum,
102    min_limit_inclusive: bool,
103    max_limit_inclusive: bool,
104) -> NumericLineEdit:
105    """Create a numeric line edit suitable for range-slider dialogs.
106
107    Args:
108        value_is_int: Whether the editor should parse integer values.
109        init_value: Initial value shown in the line edit.
110        fmt: Slider display format string.
111        min_limit: Lower allowed hard limit.
112        max_limit: Upper allowed hard limit.
113        min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower limit is inclusive.
114        max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper limit is inclusive.
115
116    Returns:
117        Configured numeric line edit widget.
118    """
119    val_fmt = get_numeric_format_field(fmt)
120    return NumericLineEdit(
121        init_val=init_value,
122        value_is_int=value_is_int,
123        val_fmt=val_fmt,
124        min_limit=min_limit,
125        max_limit=max_limit,
126        min_limit_inclusive=min_limit_inclusive,
127        max_limit_inclusive=max_limit_inclusive,
128    )

Create a numeric line edit suitable for range-slider dialogs.

Args: value_is_int: Whether the editor should parse integer values. init_value: Initial value shown in the line edit. fmt: Slider display format string. min_limit: Lower allowed hard limit. max_limit: Upper allowed hard limit. min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower limit is inclusive. max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper limit is inclusive.

Returns: Configured numeric line edit widget.

class EditConfigDialog(PySide6.QtWidgets.QDialog, typing.Generic[~TNum]): 
131class EditConfigDialog(QDialog, Generic[TNum]):
132    """Dialog for editing a range slider's configuration."""
133
134    def __init__(
135        self,
136        range_slider_name: str,
137        value_is_int: bool,
138        min_val: TNum,
139        max_val: TNum,
140        fmt: str,
141        min_limit: TNum | None,
142        max_limit: TNum | None,
143        min_limit_inclusive: bool,
144        max_limit_inclusive: bool,
145        show_range: bool = True,
146        show_range_enabled: bool = True,
147        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,
148    ):
149        """Initialize the configuration dialog.
150
151        Args:
152            range_slider_name: Display name of the range slider.
153            value_is_int: Whether the slider uses integer values.
154            min_val: Current minimum value.
155            max_val: Current maximum value.
156            fmt: Current display format string.
157            min_limit: Optional lower hard limit.
158            max_limit: Optional upper hard limit.
159            min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower hard limit is inclusive.
160            max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper hard limit is inclusive.
161            show_range: Initial state of the range-visibility option.
162            show_range_enabled: Whether the range-visibility option is editable.
163            parent: Optional parent widget.
164        """
165        super().__init__(parent)
166        self.setWindowTitle("Edit Configuration")
167
168        self._show_range = show_range
169        self._show_range_enabled = show_range_enabled
170        self._value_is_int = value_is_int
171        self._edit_min = make_line_edit(
172            value_is_int,
173            min_val,
174            fmt,
175            min_limit,
176            max_limit,
177            min_limit_inclusive,
178            max_limit_inclusive,
179        )
180        self._edit_max = make_line_edit(
181            value_is_int,
182            max_val,
183            fmt,
184            min_limit,
185            max_limit,
186            min_limit_inclusive,
187            max_limit_inclusive,
188        )
189        self._edit_fmt = QLineEdit(fmt)
190        self._cb_ticks = QCheckBox("Show Range", self)
191        self._cb_ticks.setChecked(show_range)
192        self._cb_ticks.setEnabled(show_range_enabled)
193
194        self._edit_fmt.editingFinished.connect(self._fmt_changed)
195
196        buttons = QDialogButtonBox(QDialogButtonBox.Ok | QDialogButtonBox.Cancel, parent=self)
197        buttons.accepted.connect(self.accept)
198        buttons.rejected.connect(self.reject)
199
200        form = QFormLayout(self)
201        form.addRow("Range Slider:", SvgLabel(range_slider_name, alignment=Qt.AlignLeft | Qt.AlignVCenter))
202        form.addRow(self._cb_ticks)
203        form.addRow("Min:", self._edit_min)
204        form.addRow("Max:", self._edit_max)
205        form.addRow("Format:", self._edit_fmt)
206        form.addRow(buttons)
207
208        self.setSizeGripEnabled(False)
209        self.adjustSize()
210        self.setFixedSize(self.size())
211
212    def _fmt_changed(self) -> None:
213        """Update numeric validators after the format field changes."""
214        fmt = get_numeric_format_field(self._edit_fmt.text())
215        self._edit_min.set_fmt(fmt)
216        self._edit_max.set_fmt(fmt)
217
218    def accept(self) -> None:
219        """Validate the dialog state and accept it when valid."""
220        range_min = self._edit_min.get_value()
221        range_max = self._edit_max.get_value()
222
223        if range_max <= range_min:
224            show_warning("Invalid range", "Min must be less than Max", parent=self)
225            return
226
227        super().accept()
228
229    def get_values(self) -> Tuple[TNum, TNum, str, bool]:
230        """Return the validated dialog values.
231
232        Returns:
233            Tuple containing minimum value, maximum value, format string, and
234            range-visibility flag.
235        """
236        return (
237            self._edit_min.get_value(),
238            self._edit_max.get_value(),
239            self._edit_fmt.text(),
240            self._cb_ticks.isChecked(),
241        )
242
243    def showEvent(self, event: QShowEvent) -> None:
244        """Focus the minimum editor when the dialog is shown.
245
246        Args:
247            event: Qt show event.
248        """
249        super().showEvent(event)
250        self._edit_min.setFocus()
251        self._edit_min.selectAll()

Dialog for editing a range slider's configuration.

def get_values(self) -> Tuple[~TNum, ~TNum, str, bool]: 
229    def get_values(self) -> Tuple[TNum, TNum, str, bool]:
230        """Return the validated dialog values.
231
232        Returns:
233            Tuple containing minimum value, maximum value, format string, and
234            range-visibility flag.
235        """
236        return (
237            self._edit_min.get_value(),
238            self._edit_max.get_value(),
239            self._edit_fmt.text(),
240            self._cb_ticks.isChecked(),
241        )

Return the validated dialog values.

Returns: Tuple containing minimum value, maximum value, format string, and range-visibility flag.

class SetValuesDialog(PySide6.QtWidgets.QDialog, typing.Generic[~TNum]): 
254class SetValuesDialog(QDialog, Generic[TNum]):
255    """Dialog for editing a lower and upper bound pair."""
256
257    def __init__(
258        self,
259        slider_name: str,
260        value_is_int: bool,
261        current_lower: TNum,
262        current_upper: TNum,
263        fmt: str,
264        min_limit: TNum | None,
265        max_limit: TNum | None,
266        min_limit_inclusive: bool,
267        max_limit_inclusive: bool,
268        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,
269    ):
270        """Initialize the value-pair dialog.
271
272        Args:
273            slider_name: Display name of the slider.
274            value_is_int: Whether the slider uses integer values.
275            current_lower: Current lower bound.
276            current_upper: Current upper bound.
277            fmt: Current display format string.
278            min_limit: Optional lower hard limit.
279            max_limit: Optional upper hard limit.
280            min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower hard limit is inclusive.
281            max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper hard limit is inclusive.
282            parent: Optional parent widget.
283        """
284        super().__init__(parent)
285        self.setWindowTitle("Set Values")
286
287        self._edit_lower = make_line_edit(
288            value_is_int,
289            current_lower,
290            fmt,
291            min_limit,
292            max_limit,
293            min_limit_inclusive,
294            max_limit_inclusive,
295        )
296        self._edit_upper = make_line_edit(
297            value_is_int,
298            current_upper,
299            fmt,
300            min_limit,
301            max_limit,
302            min_limit_inclusive,
303            max_limit_inclusive,
304        )
305
306        buttons = QDialogButtonBox(QDialogButtonBox.Ok | QDialogButtonBox.Cancel, parent=self)
307        buttons.accepted.connect(self.accept)
308        buttons.rejected.connect(self.reject)
309
310        form = QFormLayout(self)
311        form.addRow("Range Slider:", SvgLabel(slider_name, alignment=Qt.AlignLeft | Qt.AlignVCenter))
312        form.addRow("Lower:", self._edit_lower)
313        form.addRow("Upper:", self._edit_upper)
314        form.addRow(buttons)
315
316        self.setSizeGripEnabled(False)
317        self.adjustSize()
318        self.setFixedSize(self.size())
319
320    def accept(self) -> None:
321        """Validate the lower and upper values before accepting."""
322        lower = self._edit_lower.get_value()
323        upper = self._edit_upper.get_value()
324
325        if upper < lower:
326            show_warning("Invalid range", "Lower must be less than or equal to upper.", parent=self)
327            return
328
329        super().accept()
330
331    def get_values(self) -> Tuple[TNum, TNum]:
332        """Return the validated lower and upper values.
333
334        Returns:
335            Tuple containing the lower and upper values.
336        """
337        return self._edit_lower.get_value(), self._edit_upper.get_value()
338
339    def showEvent(self, event: QShowEvent) -> None:
340        """Focus the lower editor when the dialog is shown.
341
342        Args:
343            event: Qt show event.
344        """
345        super().showEvent(event)
346        self._edit_lower.setFocus()
347        self._edit_lower.selectAll()

Dialog for editing a lower and upper bound pair.

def get_values(self) -> Tuple[~TNum, ~TNum]: 
331    def get_values(self) -> Tuple[TNum, TNum]:
332        """Return the validated lower and upper values.
333
334        Returns:
335            Tuple containing the lower and upper values.
336        """
337        return self._edit_lower.get_value(), self._edit_upper.get_value()

Return the validated lower and upper values.

Returns: Tuple containing the lower and upper values.

class SetMarginsDialog(PySide6.QtWidgets.QDialog): 
350class SetMarginsDialog(QDialog):
351    """Dialog for editing lower and upper margin values."""
352
353    def __init__(
354        self,
355        slider_name: str,
356        margin_lower: int,
357        margin_upper: int,
358        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,
359    ):
360        """Initialize the margins dialog.
361
362        Args:
363            slider_name: Display name of the slider.
364            margin_lower: Current lower margin.
365            margin_upper: Current upper margin.
366            parent: Optional parent widget.
367        """
368        super().__init__(parent)
369        self.setWindowTitle("Set Margins")
370
371        self._margin_lo_sb = self._build_spin_box(margin_lower)
372        self._margin_up_sb = self._build_spin_box(margin_upper)
373
374        buttons = QDialogButtonBox(QDialogButtonBox.Ok | QDialogButtonBox.Cancel, parent=self)
375        buttons.accepted.connect(self.accept)
376        buttons.rejected.connect(self.reject)
377
378        form = QFormLayout(self)
379        form.addRow("Range Slider:", SvgLabel(slider_name, alignment=Qt.AlignLeft | Qt.AlignVCenter))
380        form.addRow("Upper:", self._margin_up_sb)
381        form.addRow("Lower:", self._margin_lo_sb)
382        form.addRow(buttons)
383
384        self.setSizeGripEnabled(False)
385        self.adjustSize()
386        self.setFixedSize(self.size())
387
388    def _build_spin_box(self, value: int) -> QSpinBox:
389        """Create a spin box configured for margin editing.
390
391        Args:
392            value: Initial spin-box value.
393
394        Returns:
395            Configured spin box.
396        """
397        sb = QSpinBox(self)
398        sb.setSuffix(" %")
399        sb.setMinimum(0)
400        sb.setMaximum(100)
401        sb.setValue(value)
402        sb.setFixedWidth(sb.sizeHint().width())
403        return sb
404
405    def get_values(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
406        """Return the validated margin values.
407
408        Returns:
409            Tuple containing lower and upper margins.
410        """
411        return self._margin_lo_sb.value(), self._margin_up_sb.value()
412
413    def showEvent(self, event: QShowEvent) -> None:
414        """Focus the lower-margin editor when the dialog is shown.
415
416        Args:
417            event: Qt show event.
418        """
419        super().showEvent(event)
420        self._margin_lo_sb.setFocus()
421        self._margin_lo_sb.selectAll()

Dialog for editing lower and upper margin values.

SetMarginsDialog( slider_name: str, margin_lower: int, margin_upper: int, parent: Optional[PySide6.QtWidgets.QWidget] = None) 
353    def __init__(
354        self,
355        slider_name: str,
356        margin_lower: int,
357        margin_upper: int,
358        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,
359    ):
360        """Initialize the margins dialog.
361
362        Args:
363            slider_name: Display name of the slider.
364            margin_lower: Current lower margin.
365            margin_upper: Current upper margin.
366            parent: Optional parent widget.
367        """
368        super().__init__(parent)
369        self.setWindowTitle("Set Margins")
370
371        self._margin_lo_sb = self._build_spin_box(margin_lower)
372        self._margin_up_sb = self._build_spin_box(margin_upper)
373
374        buttons = QDialogButtonBox(QDialogButtonBox.Ok | QDialogButtonBox.Cancel, parent=self)
375        buttons.accepted.connect(self.accept)
376        buttons.rejected.connect(self.reject)
377
378        form = QFormLayout(self)
379        form.addRow("Range Slider:", SvgLabel(slider_name, alignment=Qt.AlignLeft | Qt.AlignVCenter))
380        form.addRow("Upper:", self._margin_up_sb)
381        form.addRow("Lower:", self._margin_lo_sb)
382        form.addRow(buttons)
383
384        self.setSizeGripEnabled(False)
385        self.adjustSize()
386        self.setFixedSize(self.size())

Initialize the margins dialog.

Args: slider_name: Display name of the slider. margin_lower: Current lower margin. margin_upper: Current upper margin. parent: Optional parent widget.

def get_values(self) -> Tuple[int, int]: 
405    def get_values(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
406        """Return the validated margin values.
407
408        Returns:
409            Tuple containing lower and upper margins.
410        """
411        return self._margin_lo_sb.value(), self._margin_up_sb.value()

Return the validated margin values.

Returns: Tuple containing lower and upper margins.

def showEvent(self, event: PySide6.QtGui.QShowEvent) -> None: 
413    def showEvent(self, event: QShowEvent) -> None:
414        """Focus the lower-margin editor when the dialog is shown.
415
416        Args:
417            event: Qt show event.
418        """
419        super().showEvent(event)
420        self._margin_lo_sb.setFocus()
421        self._margin_lo_sb.selectAll()
