class NumericRangeSliderBase(ui.params.range_sliders.LabeledRangeSliderBase[~TNum], typing.Generic[~TNum]): 
1657class NumericRangeSliderBase(LabeledRangeSliderBase[TNum], Generic[TNum]):
1658    """Base class for integer and float range sliders."""
1659
1660    def __init__(
1661        self,
1662        label: str,
1663        unit: str,
1664        value_is_int: bool,
1665        min_val: TNum,
1666        max_val: TNum,
1667        init_vals: Optional[Tuple[TNum, TNum]],
1668        val_fmt: str,
1669        min_limit: TNum | None,
1670        max_limit: TNum | None,
1671        min_limit_inclusive: bool,
1672        max_limit_inclusive: bool,
1673        use_margins: bool,
1674        margins: Tuple[int, int],
1675        show_range: bool,
1676        orientation: Qt.Orientation,
1677        editable: bool,
1678        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,
1679    ):
1680        """Initialize the numeric range-slider base class.
1681
1682        Args:
1683            label: Slider label text.
1684            unit: Optional unit label.
1685            value_is_int: Whether the slider uses integer values.
1686            min_val: Initial minimum value.
1687            max_val: Initial maximum value.
1688            init_vals: Initial lower and upper values.
1689            val_fmt: Value display format string.
1690            min_limit: Optional lower hard limit.
1691            max_limit: Optional upper hard limit.
1692            min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower hard limit is inclusive.
1693            max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper hard limit is inclusive.
1694            use_margins: Whether the slider is currently controlled by margins.
1695            margins: Lower and upper margin values.
1696            show_range: Whether range labels and ticks should be shown.
1697            orientation: Slider orientation.
1698            editable: Whether configuration editing is enabled.
1699            parent: Optional parent widget.
1700        """
1701        super().__init__(
1702            label=label,
1703            unit=unit,
1704            min_val=min_val,
1705            max_val=max_val,
1706            init_vals=init_vals,
1707            orientation=orientation,
1708            val_fmt=val_fmt,
1709            show_range=show_range,
1710            parent=parent,
1711        )
1712
1713        self.value_is_int = value_is_int
1714        self._min_limit = min_limit
1715        self._max_limit = max_limit
1716        self._min_limit_inclusive = min_limit_inclusive
1717        self._max_limit_inclusive = max_limit_inclusive
1718        self._editable = editable
1719        self._use_margins = use_margins
1720        self._margin_lower = margins[0]
1721        self._margin_upper = margins[1]
1722        self._add_right_click_items()
1723        self._set_right_click_item_enabled("edit_config", editable)
1724
1725        self._slider.valueChanged.connect(self._on_slider_values_changed)
1726
1727        self._apply_mode()
1728        self._update_value_width()
1729        self._update_layout()
1730        self._update_all_labels()
1731
1732    def _validate_limit_one(self, name: str, value: TNum) -> None:
1733        """Validate that one value lies within hard limits.
1734
1735        Args:
1736            name: Name of the value being validated.
1737            value: Value to validate.
1738        """
1739        if self._min_limit is not None:
1740            if self._min_limit_inclusive:
1741                ok = value >= self._min_limit
1742            else:
1743                ok = value > self._min_limit
1744            if not ok:
1745                raise ValueError(f"{name} must be within the allowed limits.")
1746
1747        if self._max_limit is not None:
1748            if self._max_limit_inclusive:
1749                ok = value <= self._max_limit
1750            else:
1751                ok = value < self._max_limit
1752            if not ok:
1753                raise ValueError(f"{name} must be within the allowed limits.")
1754
1755    def _validate_pair(self, values: Tuple[Any, Any]) -> Tuple[TNum, TNum]:
1756        """Validate a lower/upper pair.
1757
1758        Args:
1759            values: Pair of proposed lower and upper values.
1760
1761        Returns:
1762            Normalized lower and upper values.
1763        """
1764        lo = self.evaluate_value(values[0])
1765        hi = self.evaluate_value(values[1])
1766
1767        self._validate_limit_one("lower", lo)
1768        self._validate_limit_one("upper", hi)
1769
1770        if hi <= lo:
1771            raise ValueError("upper must be greater than lower")
1772
1773        return lo, hi
1774
1775    def _clamp_pair_to_range(
1776        self,
1777        values: Tuple[TNum, TNum],
1778        new_min: TNum,
1779        new_max: TNum,
1780    ) -> Tuple[TNum, TNum]:
1781        """Clamp a lower/upper pair to a new numeric range.
1782
1783        Args:
1784            values: Pair of lower and upper values.
1785            new_min: New minimum range value.
1786            new_max: New maximum range value.
1787
1788        Returns:
1789            Clamped lower and upper values.
1790        """
1791        lo, hi = values
1792
1793        lo = max(lo, new_min)
1794        hi = min(hi, new_max)
1795
1796        if lo > new_max:
1797            lo = new_min
1798
1799        if hi < new_min:
1800            hi = new_max
1801
1802        return lo, hi
1803
1804    def _add_right_click_items(self):
1805        """Append range-slider-specific context-menu items."""
1806        self._right_click_items.extend(
1807            [
1808                {"id": "sep"},
1809                {"id": "use_values", "text": "Use Values", "checkable": True, "checked": not self._use_margins},
1810                {"id": "set_values", "text": "Set Values..."},
1811                {"id": "sep"},
1812                {"id": "use_margins", "text": "Use Margins", "checkable": True, "checked": self._use_margins},
1813                {"id": "set_margins", "text": "Set Margins..."},
1814                {"id": "sep"},
1815                {"id": "edit_config", "text": "Edit Configuration...", "enabled": True},
1816            ]
1817        )
1818
1819    def _right_click_requested(self, item_id, checked: bool) -> None:
1820        """Dispatch range-slider-specific context-menu actions.
1821
1822        Args:
1823            item_id: Triggered menu item identifier.
1824            checked: Checked state associated with the action.
1825        """
1826        super()._right_click_requested(item_id, checked)
1827        if item_id == "edit_config":
1828            self._open_edit_config_dlg()
1829        elif item_id == "set_values":
1830            self._open_set_values_dlg()
1831        elif item_id == "use_values":
1832            if checked:
1833                self._use_margins = False
1834                self._apply_mode()
1835            else:
1836                self._set_right_click_item_checked("use_values", True)
1837        elif item_id == "use_margins":
1838            if checked:
1839                self._use_margins = True
1840                self._apply_mode()
1841            else:
1842                self._set_right_click_item_checked("use_margins", True)
1843        elif item_id == "set_margins":
1844            self._open_set_margins_dialog()
1845
1846    def _open_edit_config_dlg(self) -> None:
1847        """Open the configuration dialog and apply accepted changes."""
1848        dlg = EditConfigDialog[TNum](
1849            range_slider_name=self._label_text,
1850            value_is_int=self.value_is_int,
1851            min_val=self._min_value,
1852            max_val=self._max_value,
1853            fmt=self._value_fmt,
1854            show_range=self._show_range,
1855            min_limit=self._min_limit,
1856            max_limit=self._max_limit,
1857            min_limit_inclusive=self._min_limit_inclusive,
1858            max_limit_inclusive=self._max_limit_inclusive,
1859        )
1860        if dlg.exec() != QDialog.Accepted:
1861            return
1862
1863        new_min, new_max, new_fmt, new_show_range = dlg.get_values()
1864
1865        old_min = self._min_value
1866        old_max = self._max_value
1867        old_fmt = self._value_fmt
1868        old_show_range = self._show_range
1869
1870        new_min = self.evaluate_value(new_min)
1871        new_max = self.evaluate_value(new_max)
1872
1873        self.set_format(new_fmt)
1874
1875        new_cur = self._clamp_pair_to_range((self._current_lower, self._current_upper), new_min, new_max)
1876        new_dft = self._clamp_pair_to_range((self._default_lower, self._default_upper), new_min, new_max)
1877
1878        needs_clamp = (
1879            new_cur != (self._current_lower, self._current_upper)
1880            or new_dft != (self._default_lower, self._default_upper)
1881        )
1882
1883        if needs_clamp:
1884            self.set_border(SliderBorderState.ERROR)
1885            try:
1886                result = range_slider_ask_clamp_value(
1887                    proposed_range=self._range_to_text((new_min, new_max)),
1888                    old_cur=self._range_to_text((self._current_lower, self._current_upper)),
1889                    old_dft=self._range_to_text((self._default_lower, self._default_upper)),
1890                    new_cur=self._range_to_text(new_cur),
1891                    new_dft=self._range_to_text(new_dft),
1892                    parent=self,
1893                )
1894            finally:
1895                self.set_border(SliderBorderState.OFF)
1896
1897            if result != AskResult.YES:
1898                self.set_format(old_fmt)
1899                return
1900
1901        self._show_range = new_show_range
1902        self.set_range(new_min, new_max)
1903        self._update_layout()
1904
1905        if (
1906            old_min != self._min_value
1907            or old_max != self._max_value
1908            or old_fmt != self._value_fmt
1909            or old_show_range != self._show_range
1910        ):
1911            self.configChanged.emit(self.get_config())
1912
1913    def _open_set_values_dlg(self) -> None:
1914        """Open the value-pair dialog and apply accepted changes."""
1915        dlg = SetValuesDialog[TNum](
1916            slider_name=self._label_text,
1917            value_is_int=self.value_is_int,
1918            current_lower=self._current_lower,
1919            current_upper=self._current_upper,
1920            fmt=self._value_fmt,
1921            min_limit=self._min_limit,
1922            max_limit=self._max_limit,
1923            min_limit_inclusive=self._min_limit_inclusive,
1924            max_limit_inclusive=self._max_limit_inclusive,
1925        )
1926        if dlg.exec() != QDialog.Accepted:
1927            return
1928
1929        new_pair = self._validate_pair(dlg.get_values())
1930        old_pair = self.get_value()
1931        old_default = self.get_default_value()
1932
1933        needs_range_change = not (
1934            self._min_value <= new_pair[0] <= self._max_value
1935            and self._min_value <= new_pair[1] <= self._max_value
1936        )
1937
1938        if needs_range_change:
1939            new_min = min(self._min_value, new_pair[0])
1940            new_max = max(self._max_value, new_pair[1])
1941
1942            self.set_border(SliderBorderState.ERROR)
1943            try:
1944                result = range_slider_ask_extend_range(
1945                    proposed_vals=self._range_to_text(new_pair),
1946                    old_range=self._range_to_text((self._max_value, self._max_value)),
1947                    new_range=self._range_to_text((new_min, new_max)),
1948                    parent=self,
1949                )
1950            finally:
1951                self.set_border(SliderBorderState.OFF)
1952            if result != AskResult.YES:
1953                return
1954
1955        self.set_value({"range": new_pair})
1956
1957        if old_pair != self.get_value():
1958            self.valueChanged.emit(self.get_value())
1959        if old_default != self.get_default_value():
1960            self.defaultChanged.emit(self.get_default_value())
1961
1962    def _apply_mode(self):
1963        """Apply the current values-vs-margins operating mode."""
1964        use_margins = self._use_margins
1965        self._set_right_click_item_checked("use_margins", use_margins)
1966        self._set_right_click_item_checked("use_values", not use_margins)
1967
1968        self._set_right_click_item_enabled("set_margins", use_margins)
1969        self._set_right_click_item_enabled("set_values", not use_margins)
1970        self._set_right_click_item_enabled("save_as_default", not use_margins)
1971        self._set_right_click_item_enabled("reset_to_default", not use_margins)
1972
1973        widgets = [
1974            self._slider,
1975            self._min_label,
1976            self._max_label,
1977            self._value_wrap,
1978        ]
1979
1980        for w in widgets:
1981            w.setEnabled(not use_margins)
1982
1983        if hasattr(self, "_right_click_overlay"):
1984            self._right_click_overlay.sync_to_target()
1985
1986        self.valueChanged.emit(self.get_value())
1987
1988    def _open_set_margins_dialog(self) -> None:
1989        """Open the margins dialog and apply accepted changes."""
1990        dlg = SetMarginsDialog(
1991            slider_name=self._label_text,
1992            margin_lower=self._margin_lower,
1993            margin_upper=self._margin_upper,
1994        )
1995        if dlg.exec() != QDialog.Accepted:
1996            return
1997
1998        new_margin_lo, new_margin_up = dlg.get_values()
1999
2000        if not (new_margin_lo == self._margin_lower and new_margin_up == self._margin_upper):
2001            self._margin_lower = new_margin_lo
2002            self._margin_upper = new_margin_up
2003            self.valueChanged.emit(self.get_value())
2004
2005    def get_config(self) -> dict:
2006        """Return the current configuration mapping.
2007
2008        Returns:
2009            Configuration dictionary containing numeric bounds, display format,
2010            and range-visibility state.
2011        """
2012        return {
2013            "min_val": self._min_value,
2014            "max_val": self._max_value,
2015            "val_fmt": self._value_fmt,
2016            "show_range": self._show_range,
2017        }
2018
2019    def _apply_config(self, config: dict) -> None:
2020        """Apply a validated configuration mapping.
2021
2022        Args:
2023            config: Configuration mapping to apply.
2024        """
2025        self._show_range = config["show_range"]
2026        self.set_format(config["val_fmt"])
2027        self.set_range(config["min_val"], config["max_val"])
2028
2029    def get_value(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2030        """Return the current values-and-margins state.
2031
2032        Returns:
2033            Mapping containing the active mode, finite range values, and margins.
2034        """
2035        return {
2036            "use_margins": self._use_margins,
2037            "range": (self._current_lower, self._current_upper),
2038            "margins": (self._margin_lower, self._margin_upper),
2039        }
2040
2041    def get_current_value(self) -> Tuple[TNum, TNum]:
2042        """Return the current finite lower and upper values."""
2043        return self._current_lower, self._current_upper
2044
2045    def get_default_value(self) -> Tuple[TNum, TNum]:
2046        """Return the stored default lower and upper values."""
2047        return self._default_lower, self._default_upper
2048
2049    def get_min_value(self) -> TNum:
2050        """Return the current minimum range value."""
2051        return self._min_value
2052
2053    def get_max_value(self) -> TNum:
2054        """Return the current maximum range value."""
2055        return self._max_value
2056
2057    def set_value(self, value: Dict[str, Any], keep_default_value: bool = False) -> None:
2058        """Set the current state of the range slider.
2059
2060        Args:
2061            value: Mapping containing ``use_margins``, ``range``, and
2062                optionally ``margins``.
2063            keep_default_value: Whether to preserve the stored default range.
2064        """
2065        self._use_margins = value.get("use_margins", self._use_margins)
2066        self._set_right_click_item_checked("use_margins", self._use_margins)
2067        self._set_right_click_item_checked("use_values", not self._use_margins)
2068        range_values = value.get("range", (self._current_lower, self._current_upper))
2069        self._margin_lower, self._margin_upper = value.get(
2070            "margins",
2071            (self._margin_lower, self._margin_upper),
2072        )
2073        lo, hi = self._validate_pair(range_values)
2074        self._current_lower = lo
2075        self._current_upper = hi
2076        if not keep_default_value:
2077            self._default_lower = lo
2078            self._default_upper = hi
2079
2080        cfg_changed = False
2081        if lo < self._min_value:
2082            self._min_value = lo
2083            cfg_changed = True
2084        if hi > self._max_value:
2085            self._max_value = hi
2086            cfg_changed = True
2087
2088        self._slider.blockSignals(True)
2089        try:
2090            self._slider.setRange(float(self._min_value), float(self._max_value))
2091            self._slider.setValues((float(lo), float(hi)))
2092        finally:
2093            self._slider.blockSignals(False)
2094
2095        self._update_all_labels()
2096        self._update_value_width()
2097        self._apply_mode()
2098
2099        if cfg_changed:
2100            self.configChanged.emit(self.get_config())
2101
2102    def set_default_value(self, value: Tuple[TNum, TNum]) -> None:
2103        """Set the stored default lower and upper values.
2104
2105        Args:
2106            value: New default lower and upper values.
2107        """
2108        lo, hi = self._validate_pair(value)
2109        self._default_lower = lo
2110        self._default_upper = hi
2111
2112        cfg_changed = False
2113        if lo < self._min_value:
2114            self._min_value = lo
2115            cfg_changed = True
2116        if hi > self._max_value:
2117            self._max_value = hi
2118            cfg_changed = True
2119
2120        self._slider.blockSignals(True)
2121        try:
2122            self._slider.setRange(float(self._min_value), float(self._max_value))
2123            self._slider.setValues((float(self._current_lower), float(self._current_upper)))
2124        finally:
2125            self._slider.blockSignals(False)
2126
2127        self._update_all_labels()
2128        self._update_value_width()
2129
2130        if cfg_changed:
2131            self.configChanged.emit(self.get_config())
2132
2133    def set_range(self, min_val: TNum, max_val: TNum) -> None:
2134        """Set the finite numeric range.
2135
2136        Args:
2137            min_val: New minimum value.
2138            max_val: New maximum value.
2139        """
2140        min_val = self.evaluate_value(min_val)
2141        max_val = self.evaluate_value(max_val)
2142
2143        self._validate_limit_one("min", min_val)
2144        self._validate_limit_one("max", max_val)
2145
2146        if max_val < min_val:
2147            raise ValueError("max_val must be greater than min_val")
2148
2149        self._min_value = min_val
2150        self._max_value = max_val
2151
2152        new_cur_lo, new_cur_up = self._clamp_pair_to_range(
2153            (self._current_lower, self._current_upper),
2154            min_val,
2155            max_val,
2156        )
2157        cur_clamped = False
2158        if new_cur_lo != self._current_lower or new_cur_up != self._current_upper:
2159            self._current_lower = new_cur_lo
2160            self._current_upper = new_cur_up
2161            cur_clamped = True
2162
2163        new_dft_lo, new_dft_up = self._clamp_pair_to_range(
2164            (self._default_lower, self._default_upper),
2165            min_val,
2166            max_val,
2167        )
2168        dft_clamped = False
2169        if new_dft_lo != self._default_lower or new_dft_up != self._default_upper:
2170            self._default_lower = new_dft_lo
2171            self._default_upper = new_dft_up
2172            dft_clamped = True
2173
2174        self._slider.blockSignals(True)
2175        try:
2176            self._slider.setRange(float(min_val), float(max_val))
2177            self._slider.setValues((float(self._current_lower), float(self._current_upper)))
2178        finally:
2179            self._slider.blockSignals(False)
2180
2181        self._update_all_labels()
2182        self._update_value_width()
2183
2184        if cur_clamped:
2185            self.valueChanged.emit(self.get_value())
2186
2187        if dft_clamped:
2188            self.defaultChanged.emit(self.get_default_value())
2189
2190    def set_min_value(self, min_val: TNum) -> None:
2191        """Set only the minimum bound.
2192
2193        Args:
2194            min_val: New minimum value.
2195        """
2196        self.set_range(min_val, self._max_value)
2197
2198    def set_max_value(self, max_val: TNum) -> None:
2199        """Set only the maximum bound.
2200
2201        Args:
2202            max_val: New maximum value.
2203        """
2204        self.set_range(self._min_value, max_val)

Base class for integer and float range sliders.

NumericRangeSliderBase( label: str, unit: str, value_is_int: bool, min_val: ~TNum, max_val: ~TNum, init_vals: Optional[Tuple[~TNum, ~TNum]], val_fmt: str, min_limit: Optional[~TNum], max_limit: Optional[~TNum], min_limit_inclusive: bool, max_limit_inclusive: bool, use_margins: bool, margins: Tuple[int, int], show_range: bool, orientation: PySide6.QtCore.Qt.Orientation, editable: bool, parent: Optional[PySide6.QtWidgets.QWidget] = None) 
1660    def __init__(
1661        self,
1662        label: str,
1663        unit: str,
1664        value_is_int: bool,
1665        min_val: TNum,
1666        max_val: TNum,
1667        init_vals: Optional[Tuple[TNum, TNum]],
1668        val_fmt: str,
1669        min_limit: TNum | None,
1670        max_limit: TNum | None,
1671        min_limit_inclusive: bool,
1672        max_limit_inclusive: bool,
1673        use_margins: bool,
1674        margins: Tuple[int, int],
1675        show_range: bool,
1676        orientation: Qt.Orientation,
1677        editable: bool,
1678        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,
1679    ):
1680        """Initialize the numeric range-slider base class.
1681
1682        Args:
1683            label: Slider label text.
1684            unit: Optional unit label.
1685            value_is_int: Whether the slider uses integer values.
1686            min_val: Initial minimum value.
1687            max_val: Initial maximum value.
1688            init_vals: Initial lower and upper values.
1689            val_fmt: Value display format string.
1690            min_limit: Optional lower hard limit.
1691            max_limit: Optional upper hard limit.
1692            min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower hard limit is inclusive.
1693            max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper hard limit is inclusive.
1694            use_margins: Whether the slider is currently controlled by margins.
1695            margins: Lower and upper margin values.
1696            show_range: Whether range labels and ticks should be shown.
1697            orientation: Slider orientation.
1698            editable: Whether configuration editing is enabled.
1699            parent: Optional parent widget.
1700        """
1701        super().__init__(
1702            label=label,
1703            unit=unit,
1704            min_val=min_val,
1705            max_val=max_val,
1706            init_vals=init_vals,
1707            orientation=orientation,
1708            val_fmt=val_fmt,
1709            show_range=show_range,
1710            parent=parent,
1711        )
1712
1713        self.value_is_int = value_is_int
1714        self._min_limit = min_limit
1715        self._max_limit = max_limit
1716        self._min_limit_inclusive = min_limit_inclusive
1717        self._max_limit_inclusive = max_limit_inclusive
1718        self._editable = editable
1719        self._use_margins = use_margins
1720        self._margin_lower = margins[0]
1721        self._margin_upper = margins[1]
1722        self._add_right_click_items()
1723        self._set_right_click_item_enabled("edit_config", editable)
1724
1725        self._slider.valueChanged.connect(self._on_slider_values_changed)
1726
1727        self._apply_mode()
1728        self._update_value_width()
1729        self._update_layout()
1730        self._update_all_labels()

Initialize the numeric range-slider base class.

Args: label: Slider label text. unit: Optional unit label. value_is_int: Whether the slider uses integer values. min_val: Initial minimum value. max_val: Initial maximum value. init_vals: Initial lower and upper values. val_fmt: Value display format string. min_limit: Optional lower hard limit. max_limit: Optional upper hard limit. min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower hard limit is inclusive. max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper hard limit is inclusive. use_margins: Whether the slider is currently controlled by margins. margins: Lower and upper margin values. show_range: Whether range labels and ticks should be shown. orientation: Slider orientation. editable: Whether configuration editing is enabled. parent: Optional parent widget.

value_is_int
def get_config(self) -> dict: 
2005    def get_config(self) -> dict:
2006        """Return the current configuration mapping.
2007
2008        Returns:
2009            Configuration dictionary containing numeric bounds, display format,
2010            and range-visibility state.
2011        """
2012        return {
2013            "min_val": self._min_value,
2014            "max_val": self._max_value,
2015            "val_fmt": self._value_fmt,
2016            "show_range": self._show_range,
2017        }

Return the current configuration mapping.

Returns: Configuration dictionary containing numeric bounds, display format, and range-visibility state.

def get_value(self) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
2029    def get_value(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
2030        """Return the current values-and-margins state.
2031
2032        Returns:
2033            Mapping containing the active mode, finite range values, and margins.
2034        """
2035        return {
2036            "use_margins": self._use_margins,
2037            "range": (self._current_lower, self._current_upper),
2038            "margins": (self._margin_lower, self._margin_upper),
2039        }

Return the current values-and-margins state.

Returns: Mapping containing the active mode, finite range values, and margins.

def get_current_value(self) -> Tuple[~TNum, ~TNum]: 
2041    def get_current_value(self) -> Tuple[TNum, TNum]:
2042        """Return the current finite lower and upper values."""
2043        return self._current_lower, self._current_upper

Return the current finite lower and upper values.

def get_default_value(self) -> Tuple[~TNum, ~TNum]: 
2045    def get_default_value(self) -> Tuple[TNum, TNum]:
2046        """Return the stored default lower and upper values."""
2047        return self._default_lower, self._default_upper

Return the stored default lower and upper values.

def get_min_value(self) -> ~TNum: 
2049    def get_min_value(self) -> TNum:
2050        """Return the current minimum range value."""
2051        return self._min_value

Return the current minimum range value.

def get_max_value(self) -> ~TNum: 
2053    def get_max_value(self) -> TNum:
2054        """Return the current maximum range value."""
2055        return self._max_value

Return the current maximum range value.

def set_value(self, value: Dict[str, Any], keep_default_value: bool = False) -> None: 
2057    def set_value(self, value: Dict[str, Any], keep_default_value: bool = False) -> None:
2058        """Set the current state of the range slider.
2059
2060        Args:
2061            value: Mapping containing ``use_margins``, ``range``, and
2062                optionally ``margins``.
2063            keep_default_value: Whether to preserve the stored default range.
2064        """
2065        self._use_margins = value.get("use_margins", self._use_margins)
2066        self._set_right_click_item_checked("use_margins", self._use_margins)
2067        self._set_right_click_item_checked("use_values", not self._use_margins)
2068        range_values = value.get("range", (self._current_lower, self._current_upper))
2069        self._margin_lower, self._margin_upper = value.get(
2070            "margins",
2071            (self._margin_lower, self._margin_upper),
2072        )
2073        lo, hi = self._validate_pair(range_values)
2074        self._current_lower = lo
2075        self._current_upper = hi
2076        if not keep_default_value:
2077            self._default_lower = lo
2078            self._default_upper = hi
2079
2080        cfg_changed = False
2081        if lo < self._min_value:
2082            self._min_value = lo
2083            cfg_changed = True
2084        if hi > self._max_value:
2085            self._max_value = hi
2086            cfg_changed = True
2087
2088        self._slider.blockSignals(True)
2089        try:
2090            self._slider.setRange(float(self._min_value), float(self._max_value))
2091            self._slider.setValues((float(lo), float(hi)))
2092        finally:
2093            self._slider.blockSignals(False)
2094
2095        self._update_all_labels()
2096        self._update_value_width()
2097        self._apply_mode()
2098
2099        if cfg_changed:
2100            self.configChanged.emit(self.get_config())

Set the current state of the range slider.

Args: value: Mapping containing use_margins, range, and optionally margins. keep_default_value: Whether to preserve the stored default range.

def set_default_value(self, value: Tuple[~TNum, ~TNum]) -> None: 
2102    def set_default_value(self, value: Tuple[TNum, TNum]) -> None:
2103        """Set the stored default lower and upper values.
2104
2105        Args:
2106            value: New default lower and upper values.
2107        """
2108        lo, hi = self._validate_pair(value)
2109        self._default_lower = lo
2110        self._default_upper = hi
2111
2112        cfg_changed = False
2113        if lo < self._min_value:
2114            self._min_value = lo
2115            cfg_changed = True
2116        if hi > self._max_value:
2117            self._max_value = hi
2118            cfg_changed = True
2119
2120        self._slider.blockSignals(True)
2121        try:
2122            self._slider.setRange(float(self._min_value), float(self._max_value))
2123            self._slider.setValues((float(self._current_lower), float(self._current_upper)))
2124        finally:
2125            self._slider.blockSignals(False)
2126
2127        self._update_all_labels()
2128        self._update_value_width()
2129
2130        if cfg_changed:
2131            self.configChanged.emit(self.get_config())

Set the stored default lower and upper values.

Args: value: New default lower and upper values.

def set_range(self, min_val: ~TNum, max_val: ~TNum) -> None: 
2133    def set_range(self, min_val: TNum, max_val: TNum) -> None:
2134        """Set the finite numeric range.
2135
2136        Args:
2137            min_val: New minimum value.
2138            max_val: New maximum value.
2139        """
2140        min_val = self.evaluate_value(min_val)
2141        max_val = self.evaluate_value(max_val)
2142
2143        self._validate_limit_one("min", min_val)
2144        self._validate_limit_one("max", max_val)
2145
2146        if max_val < min_val:
2147            raise ValueError("max_val must be greater than min_val")
2148
2149        self._min_value = min_val
2150        self._max_value = max_val
2151
2152        new_cur_lo, new_cur_up = self._clamp_pair_to_range(
2153            (self._current_lower, self._current_upper),
2154            min_val,
2155            max_val,
2156        )
2157        cur_clamped = False
2158        if new_cur_lo != self._current_lower or new_cur_up != self._current_upper:
2159            self._current_lower = new_cur_lo
2160            self._current_upper = new_cur_up
2161            cur_clamped = True
2162
2163        new_dft_lo, new_dft_up = self._clamp_pair_to_range(
2164            (self._default_lower, self._default_upper),
2165            min_val,
2166            max_val,
2167        )
2168        dft_clamped = False
2169        if new_dft_lo != self._default_lower or new_dft_up != self._default_upper:
2170            self._default_lower = new_dft_lo
2171            self._default_upper = new_dft_up
2172            dft_clamped = True
2173
2174        self._slider.blockSignals(True)
2175        try:
2176            self._slider.setRange(float(min_val), float(max_val))
2177            self._slider.setValues((float(self._current_lower), float(self._current_upper)))
2178        finally:
2179            self._slider.blockSignals(False)
2180
2181        self._update_all_labels()
2182        self._update_value_width()
2183
2184        if cur_clamped:
2185            self.valueChanged.emit(self.get_value())
2186
2187        if dft_clamped:
2188            self.defaultChanged.emit(self.get_default_value())

Set the finite numeric range.

Args: min_val: New minimum value. max_val: New maximum value.

def set_min_value(self, min_val: ~TNum) -> None: 
2190    def set_min_value(self, min_val: TNum) -> None:
2191        """Set only the minimum bound.
2192
2193        Args:
2194            min_val: New minimum value.
2195        """
2196        self.set_range(min_val, self._max_value)

Set only the minimum bound.

Args: min_val: New minimum value.

def set_max_value(self, max_val: ~TNum) -> None: 
2198    def set_max_value(self, max_val: TNum) -> None:
2199        """Set only the maximum bound.
2200
2201        Args:
2202            max_val: New maximum value.
2203        """
2204        self.set_range(self._min_value, max_val)

Set only the maximum bound.

Args: max_val: New maximum value.

class IntRangeSlider(ui.params.range_sliders.NumericRangeSliderBase[int]): 
2207class IntRangeSlider(NumericRangeSliderBase[int]):
2208    """Integer-valued range slider widget."""
2209
2210    def __init__(
2211        self,
2212        label: str,
2213        min_val: int,
2214        max_val: int,
2215        unit: str = "",
2216        init_vals: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = None,
2217        val_fmt: str = "{:d}",
2218        min_limit: int | None = None,
2219        max_limit: int | None = None,
2220        min_limit_inclusive: bool = True,
2221        max_limit_inclusive: bool = True,
2222        use_margins: bool = False,
2223        margins: Tuple[int, int] = (10, 10),
2224        show_range: bool = True,
2225        orientation: Qt.Orientation = Qt.Horizontal,
2226        editable: bool = True,
2227        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,
2228    ):
2229        """Initialize the integer range slider.
2230
2231        Args:
2232            label: Slider label text.
2233            min_val: Minimum finite value.
2234            max_val: Maximum finite value.
2235            unit: Optional unit label.
2236            init_vals: Initial lower and upper values.
2237            val_fmt: Integer display format string.
2238            min_limit: Optional lower hard limit.
2239            max_limit: Optional upper hard limit.
2240            min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower hard limit is inclusive.
2241            max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper hard limit is inclusive.
2242            use_margins: Whether the slider starts in margins mode.
2243            margins: Initial lower and upper margins.
2244            show_range: Whether range labels and ticks should be shown.
2245            orientation: Slider orientation.
2246            editable: Whether configuration editing is enabled.
2247            parent: Optional parent widget.
2248        """
2249        super().__init__(
2250            label=label,
2251            unit=unit,
2252            value_is_int=True,
2253            min_val=min_val,
2254            max_val=max_val,
2255            init_vals=init_vals,
2256            val_fmt=val_fmt,
2257            min_limit=min_limit,
2258            max_limit=max_limit,
2259            min_limit_inclusive=min_limit_inclusive,
2260            max_limit_inclusive=max_limit_inclusive,
2261            use_margins=use_margins,
2262            margins=margins,
2263            show_range=show_range,
2264            orientation=orientation,
2265            editable=editable,
2266            parent=parent,
2267        )
2268
2269    def evaluate_value(self, value: int) -> int:
2270        """Normalize an integer value using the evaluation format.
2271
2272        Args:
2273            value: Integer value to normalize.
2274
2275        Returns:
2276            Normalized integer value.
2277        """
2278        try:
2279            return int(self._eval_fmt.format(value))
2280        except Exception:
2281            return int(value)

Integer-valued range slider widget.

IntRangeSlider( label: str, min_val: int, max_val: int, unit: str = '', init_vals: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = None, val_fmt: str = '{:d}', min_limit: int | None = None, max_limit: int | None = None, min_limit_inclusive: bool = True, max_limit_inclusive: bool = True, use_margins: bool = False, margins: Tuple[int, int] = (10, 10), show_range: bool = True, orientation: PySide6.QtCore.Qt.Orientation = <Orientation.Horizontal: 1>, editable: bool = True, parent: Optional[PySide6.QtWidgets.QWidget] = None)

class FloatRangeSlider(ui.params.range_sliders.NumericRangeSliderBase[float]): 
2284class FloatRangeSlider(NumericRangeSliderBase[float]):
2285    """Floating-point range slider widget."""
2286
2287    def __init__(
2288        self,
2289        label: str,
2290        min_val: float,
2291        max_val: float,
2292        unit: str = "",
2293        init_vals: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None,
2294        val_fmt: str = "{:.3f}",
2295        min_limit: float | None = None,
2296        max_limit: float | None = None,
2297        min_limit_inclusive: bool = True,
2298        max_limit_inclusive: bool = True,
2299        use_margins: bool = False,
2300        margins: Tuple[int, int] = (10, 10),
2301        show_range: bool = True,
2302        orientation: Qt.Orientation = Qt.Horizontal,
2303        editable: bool = True,
2304        parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,
2305    ):
2306        """Initialize the floating-point range slider.
2307
2308        Args:
2309            label: Slider label text.
2310            min_val: Minimum finite value.
2311            max_val: Maximum finite value.
2312            unit: Optional unit label.
2313            init_vals: Initial lower and upper values.
2314            val_fmt: Float display format string.
2315            min_limit: Optional lower hard limit.
2316            max_limit: Optional upper hard limit.
2317            min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower hard limit is inclusive.
2318            max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper hard limit is inclusive.
2319            use_margins: Whether the slider starts in margins mode.
2320            margins: Initial lower and upper margins.
2321            show_range: Whether range labels and ticks should be shown.
2322            orientation: Slider orientation.
2323            editable: Whether configuration editing is enabled.
2324            parent: Optional parent widget.
2325        """
2326        super().__init__(
2327            label=label,
2328            unit=unit,
2329            value_is_int=False,
2330            min_val=min_val,
2331            max_val=max_val,
2332            init_vals=init_vals,
2333            val_fmt=val_fmt,
2334            min_limit=min_limit,
2335            max_limit=max_limit,
2336            min_limit_inclusive=min_limit_inclusive,
2337            max_limit_inclusive=max_limit_inclusive,
2338            use_margins=use_margins,
2339            margins=margins,
2340            show_range=show_range,
2341            orientation=orientation,
2342            editable=editable,
2343            parent=parent,
2344        )
2345
2346    def evaluate_value(self, value: float) -> float:
2347        """Normalize a float value using the evaluation format.
2348
2349        Args:
2350            value: Float value to normalize.
2351
2352        Returns:
2353            Normalized float value.
2354        """
2355        try:
2356            return float(self._eval_fmt.format(float(value)))
2357        except Exception:
2358            return float(value)

Floating-point range slider widget.

FloatRangeSlider( label: str, min_val: float, max_val: float, unit: str = '', init_vals: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, val_fmt: str = '{:.3f}', min_limit: float | None = None, max_limit: float | None = None, min_limit_inclusive: bool = True, max_limit_inclusive: bool = True, use_margins: bool = False, margins: Tuple[int, int] = (10, 10), show_range: bool = True, orientation: PySide6.QtCore.Qt.Orientation = <Orientation.Horizontal: 1>, editable: bool = True, parent: Optional[PySide6.QtWidgets.QWidget] = None) 
2287    def __init__(
2288        self,
2289        label: str,
2290        min_val: float,
2291        max_val: float,
2292        unit: str = "",

2294        val_fmt: str = "{:.3f}",
2295        min_limit: float | None = None,

2297        min_limit_inclusive: bool = True,

2300        margins: Tuple[int, int] = (10, 10),
2301        show_range: bool = True,
2302        orientation: Qt.Orientation = Qt.Horizontal,
2303        editable: bool = True,

2305    ):
2306        """Initialize the floating-point range slider.
2307
2308        Args:
2309            label: Slider label text.
2310            min_val: Minimum finite value.
2311            max_val: Maximum finite value.
2312            unit: Optional unit label.
2313            init_vals: Initial lower and upper values.
2314            val_fmt: Float display format string.
2315            min_limit: Optional lower hard limit.
2316            max_limit: Optional upper hard limit.
2317            min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower hard limit is inclusive.
2318            max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper hard limit is inclusive.
2319            use_margins: Whether the slider starts in margins mode.
2320            margins: Initial lower and upper margins.
2321            show_range: Whether range labels and ticks should be shown.
2322            orientation: Slider orientation.
2323            editable: Whether configuration editing is enabled.
2324            parent: Optional parent widget.
2325        """
2326        super().__init__(
2327            label=label,
2328            unit=unit,
2329            value_is_int=False,
2330            min_val=min_val,
2331            max_val=max_val,
2332            init_vals=init_vals,
2333            val_fmt=val_fmt,
2334            min_limit=min_limit,
2335            max_limit=max_limit,

2337            max_limit_inclusive=max_limit_inclusive,

2339            margins=margins,
2340            show_range=show_range,
2341            orientation=orientation,
2342            editable=editable,
2343            parent=parent,
2344        )

Initialize the floating-point range slider.

Args: label: Slider label text. min_val: Minimum finite value. max_val: Maximum finite value. unit: Optional unit label. init_vals: Initial lower and upper values. val_fmt: Float display format string. min_limit: Optional lower hard limit. max_limit: Optional upper hard limit. min_limit_inclusive: Whether the lower hard limit is inclusive. max_limit_inclusive: Whether the upper hard limit is inclusive. use_margins: Whether the slider starts in margins mode. margins: Initial lower and upper margins. show_range: Whether range labels and ticks should be shown. orientation: Slider orientation. editable: Whether configuration editing is enabled. parent: Optional parent widget.

def evaluate_value(self, value: float) -> float: 
2346    def evaluate_value(self, value: float) -> float:
2347        """Normalize a float value using the evaluation format.
2348
2349        Args:
2350            value: Float value to normalize.
2351
2352        Returns:
2353            Normalized float value.
2354        """
2355        try:
2356            return float(self._eval_fmt.format(float(value)))
2357        except Exception:
2358            return float(value)

Normalize a float value using the evaluation format.

Args: value: Float value to normalize.

Returns: Normalized float value.
